title: Battle of Kamino (Separatist–Mandalorian invasion)

Backed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] initiated a '''battle''' on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] and Separatist forces planned to eliminate the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] military resources by attacking their [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] facilities on the [[Water/Legends|water]] world.

It was assumed that there was only one cloning facility and its destruction would have been a major setback for Republic forces. This attempt was foiled by the defense put up by the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]].

{{Battle-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Protectors hit the Republic hard at the Battles of New Bomalex, New Holstice, Null, and Zaadja, as well as the Second Battle of Kamino.|The sourcebook ''Galaxy at War'' giving the Mandalorian Kamino battle its canonical name|<ref name="Galaxy at War">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>}}
Originally this battle on Kamino was mentioned in several sources as a Mandalorian attack on Kamino which took place in 20 BBY. With the release of the [[2009]] ''[[Galaxy at War]]'', the sourcebook gave the Mandalorian attack on Kamino its name—the Second Battle on Kamino—which followed the first Clone Wars [[First Battle of Kamino|Kamino battle]], which took place in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. However, in [[2010]], the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series episode "[[ARC Troopers (episode)|ARC Troopers]]" introduced another [[Battle of Kamino/Legends|battle on Kamino]]. Due to the unestablished timeline of ''The Clone Wars'', the date in which the "ARC Troopers" Kamino battle took place is not confirmed, thus conflicting with the Second Battle of Kamino's canonical name. Until a cohesive timeline is released, this article assumes that the name of the 20 BBY Mandalorian attack on Kamino is currently conjecture.
